feedback.claim: The spec does not define whether sha256_previous is updated when a notification send fails, so conforming implementations will diverge on all subsequent poll cycles after any failure.
feedback.evidence: Subsequent Cycles step 3 states 'MUST send a notification … and then set sha256_previous = sha256_current', which implies the update follows a successful send. The Failure Handling section instructs the system to log and continue polling but is entirely silent on sha256_previous. If sha256_previous is not updated the system will reattempt notification on the next cycle whenever the hash still differs; if it is updated no retry ever occurs. Both readings are consistent with the written text.
feedback.invariant_violated: State transitions must be fully specified for all execution paths so that independent implementations converge on identical behavior.
feedback.recommended_change: Add an explicit sentence to Failure Handling, for example: 'On send failure, sha256_previous MUST NOT be updated; the system will reattempt notification on the next poll cycle if the hash still differs.' Alternatively, if retry is undesired: 'On send failure, sha256_previous MUST be set to sha256_current so that no duplicate notification is attempted.'
feedback.severity_rationale: Two strictly conforming implementations will produce different observable outputs—repeated vs. one-shot notifications—after a single send failure. This is a hard determinism blocker.

feedback.claim: RFC3339 permits multiple valid string representations of the same UTC instant, so two implementations can produce different timestamp strings in the notification body and failure log for the same moment.
feedback.evidence: Notification Format specifies 'timestamp_utc: {RFC3339 timestamp}'; Failure Handling specifies 'timestamp_utc (RFC3339)' in the JSONL record. RFC3339 section 4.1 permits both 'Z' and '+00:00' for UTC offset, and section 5.6 allows optional fractional seconds with any number of digits. For the same instant, '2026-05-02T12:00:00Z', '2026-05-02T12:00:00.000Z', and '2026-05-02T12:00:00+00:00' are all valid RFC3339 strings.
feedback.invariant_violated: Output formats must be narrowed to a single canonical representation so that byte-identical output is achievable across implementations.
feedback.recommended_change: Narrow to a canonical sub-profile, for example: 'RFC3339 UTC using the Z suffix with no fractional seconds, e.g., 2026-05-02T12:00:00Z.'
feedback.severity_rationale: Semantic meaning is preserved across all valid representations; this is a string-level rather than behavioral divergence, warranting minor rather than blocker.

feedback.claim: 'Polls watch_path every poll_interval_seconds' does not state whether the interval is measured from poll start to next poll start or from poll end to next poll start.
feedback.evidence: The Behavior section states only 'The system polls watch_path every poll_interval_seconds.' Two common scheduling models—fixed-period (start-to-start) and sleep-after-completion (end-to-start)—are both consistent with this phrasing and will produce different poll timestamps whenever a poll cycle takes non-negligible time.
feedback.invariant_violated: Timing semantics must be unambiguous so that scheduling behavior is reproducible across implementations.
feedback.recommended_change: Specify the reference point, for example: 'The system schedules each poll to begin poll_interval_seconds after the start of the previous poll, independently of how long the poll itself takes.'
feedback.severity_rationale: For typical poll durations much shorter than the interval the observable difference is negligible; this is a determinism nit rather than a functional gap.
